task.title: Add latest-ADR discovery guidance and fallback behavior when ADRs do not exist.

context.problem_statement: The learning-comments prompt already asks agents to inspect latest ADRs, but it does not define a deterministic ADR discovery order, how to use an agent-start latest-ADR packet when present, or the fallback behavior when a repository has no ADRs or decision records. Learning mode depends on design context, so the prompt should make absence of ADRs explicit without blocking useful comment-only or explanation-note work.
background: AGW-044 added comment-only receipt evidence for learning-comments mode.
AGW-078 added non-developer explainability guidance while keeping learning-comments behavior-neutral.
workflows/prompts/codebase-learning-comments.md currently says to inspect ADR surfaces and latest ADRs but does not define fallback behavior when none exist.

scripts/agent_start.py already emits a latest ADR line and warns when no current ADR is found under docs/adr/; AGW-045 should make the prompt consume that signal when available rather than adding a new startup implementation.
scripts/build_context_packet.py already includes an ADR bucket for changed terms or latest design constraints.
make agent-task-status TASK_STATUS_INCLUDE_CLOSED=1 TASK_STATUS_JSON=1 at packet creation reported active_task_count 0, no hazards, no stale tasks, and no unknown-scope tasks.
make agent-token-budget at packet creation reported 103 files and 84121 estimated tokens, result passed.

make kit-status at packet creation reported installed repo-contract-kit 0.4.41, target repo version 0.2.21, runtime adapters none, and managed-file drift relative to installed kit metadata; this is source self-dogfood drift, not a blocker for source prompt work.
non_goals: Do not add or change ADR discovery behavior in scripts/agent_start.py, scripts/build_context_packet.py, or repo-contract-kit in this packet unless a focused prompt test proves a tiny source-side test helper is needed.
Do not create, update, or delete real ADR files.
Do not add an automated stale-comment scanner.
Do not change session receipt schema or strict validator behavior; AGW-044 already covers comment-only receipt enforcement.
Do not change runtime behavior, tests, dependencies, generated docs infrastructure, or source files outside prompt/docs/backlog/version/test surfaces.
Do not tell agents to fabricate architecture decisions, infer product intent as fact, or block learning work solely because no ADR exists.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep agent-workflow-kit as the source of prompt, schema, fixture, packaging, and workflow guidance for local agentic review and implementation.

area_contracts.purpose: Canonical learning-comments prompt for behavior-neutral comment and explanation-note work.
Generated Codex adapter mirror of the canonical learning-comments prompt.
Prompt index that should summarize learning-comments behavior without duplicating the full prompt.
Operator-facing prompt-kit usage guide for learning-comments flow.
Focused source tests and prompt-regression fixtures for prompt contract changes.

stop_conditions: Stop if the implementation needs a new ADR parser, startup command behavior, context-packet behavior, or repo-contract-kit install behavior.
Stop if the prompt would block all learning-comments work when no ADR exists.
Stop if the prompt would allow agents to infer or invent architectural decisions as facts.
Stop if generated Codex adapters are edited without changing canonical prompt source.
Stop if receipt/schema validation changes become necessary; that is outside AGW-045.

protected_files: /Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Codex_CodeReview/workflows/manifest.json unless prompt adapter export proves a manifest typo
/Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Codex_CodeReview/.agent-workflows/ runtime state except reading deterministic reports
generated files other than the Codex prompt adapter mirror
unrelated backlog rows and unrelated dirty work
expected_outputs: The canonical learning-comments prompt defines deterministic ADR/decision-record discovery order before comment or explanation-note claims are made.
The prompt says to consume agent-start/latest-ADR or context-packet ADR evidence when present before scanning manually.

The prompt defines no-ADR fallback behavior: record that no ADR was found, use README/docs/tests/code/config evidence, avoid fabricating architecture intent, mark uncertainty, and proceed only with evidence-backed comments or explanation notes.
The ADR Reader subagent output requires files inspected, latest/current/superseded decision state, no-ADR fallback evidence, and uncertainty.
Verification requires checking that terminology and claims align with latest decisions when present, or with fallback docs/code evidence when absent.
Docs and prompt index mention the no-ADR fallback briefly.
Generated Codex adapter mirrors the canonical prompt after make prompt-adapters-export.
Focused regression coverage checks the prompt contains ADR discovery and no-ADR fallback language.
Version/changelog and source backlog closeout are updated after validation.

acceptance_criteria.description: workflows/prompts/codebase-learning-comments.md defines a deterministic order for ADR discovery that starts from existing agent-start/context evidence when present and then scans documented ADR or ADR-like decision record locations.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect the prompt and focused prompt test.

acceptance_criteria.description: The prompt defines fallback behavior for repositories without ADRs: record no ADR found, use other source-of-truth docs/tests/code evidence, preserve uncertainty, avoid fabricated design intent, and continue only with evidence-backed comments or explanation notes.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect prompt sections and focused prompt test.

acceptance_criteria.description: ADR Reader and output sections require the agent to report latest/current/superseded decisions, source files, no-ADR fallback evidence, and uncertainty.
Docs/index surfaces make the learning-comments no-ADR fallback discoverable without duplicating the whole prompt.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect workflows/prompts/README.md, docs/using-the-prompt-kit.md, and README.md if changed.

acceptance_criteria.description: The change does not alter startup/context packet behavior, receipt schema, strict receipt validation, repo-contract-kit files, or actual ADR records.
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff --name-only compared with the allowed/protected scope.

acceptance_criteria.description: Version/changelog and source backlog rows close AGW-045 only after validation passes.
acceptance_criteria.verification: make version-check, make backlog-check, and make backlog-split-check.

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether the source checkout is clean, only expected files remain dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This changes a user-facing prompt workflow and generated Codex adapter.

known_risks: Too much ADR search detail can bloat the prompt and reduce token efficiency.
No-ADR fallback wording can accidentally permit speculative architecture claims.
Prompt-only changes can drift from generated Codex adapter output if export is skipped.
A broad implementation could unnecessarily change startup scripts or receipt validation.
stop_conditions: Implementation requires changing agent-start or context-packet ADR discovery behavior.
Implementation requires changing session receipt schema, verify_agent_receipt.py, or repo-contract-kit.
The prompt cannot define a compact no-ADR fallback without weakening evidence requirements.
Validation finds unrelated generated adapter drift or dirty work outside this packet's scope.

approval.notes: User requested implementation of the planned workflow-stack backlog items; AGW-045 is the next selected open item after AGW-043 closeout.

overlap_warnings: Refresh git status and task-status before worker edits.
Do not edit repo-contract-kit or installed kit runtime state.
Treat installed kit managed-file drift as known self-dogfood state unless it becomes actual git dirt or affects validation.

Keep the change source prompt/docs/test/backlog/version only, refresh generated Codex adapters from the canonical prompt, validate, commit and push source main, and report final clean state.
